title: rosa viscaria var. parvula
intro: Rosa viscaria var. parvula, also known as Dwarf Alpine Rose, is a small, flowering shrub native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Rosa viscaria var. parvula include its ability to promote tissue repair, reduce pain and swelling, and prevent infection. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, tinctures, and salves to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
